votes.reason: The Python code correctly computes the largest even number in the range [x, y] or -1 if none exists, which perfectly matches the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly implements the specification by computing the largest even number in the range [x, y] and checking if it matches the result, returning -1 if no such number exists, which exactly mirrors the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by finding the largest even integer in the range [x, y] or returning -1 if no such integer exists.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec. When x > y, there's no valid range so no even exists, returning -1. When x <= y, it finds the largest even by taking y if even, else y-1, and checks if it's >= x. This matches the Coq spec which requires res to be the largest even in [x,y] if one exists, or -1 otherwise. The Python logic correctly identifies the largest even candidate and validates it's in range.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. When x > y, there's no valid range so no even exists, returning -1. When x <= y, it finds the largest even by checking if y is even (use y) or odd (use y-1), then verifies this even is >= x. If so, that's the largest even in range; otherwise -1. This matches the Coq spec which requires res to be the largest even in [x,y] if one exists, or -1 otherwise.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. When x > y, there's no valid range so no even exists, returning -1. When x <= y, it finds the largest even by checking if y is even (use y) or odd (use y-1), then verifies this even is >= x. If so, that's the largest even in range; otherwise -1. This matches the Coq spec which requires res to be the largest even in [x,y] if one exists, or -1 otherwise.
Python computes the largest even in [x,y] (y if even else y-1, if >= x) or -1 if none, matching the Coq spec’s two cases; the precondition x>0 and y>0 also matches.

Preconditions match (x>0 and y>0). The Python spec computes the largest even in [x,y] as y if even else y-1, returning it if >= x, otherwise -1. This exactly matches the Coq spec: return the greatest even in [x,y] if it exists, else -1, including empty interval cases.
Python computes the largest even in [x, y] (or -1 if none), matching the Coq spec under the same precondition.
